text: Imbalanced learning is one of the main challenges in the data mining and machine learning research areas. Although significant improvements have been achieved over the last several decades, imbalanced learning is still a research area with high levels of competitiveness. Due to the limitations imposed by imbalanced class distribution on the application of machine learning and deep learning models, the research on imbalanced learning has achieved remarkable results in the past few years and it is still going on. This paper reviews some of the recent achievements in the area of imbalanced learning. Different from the existing surveys that are limited to the classification task in traditional machine learning, our paper also covers some methods for regression tasks and some aspects of deep long-tail learning. We discuss real-world applications of imbalanced learning and also indicate a wide range of research works based on imbalanced learning from management science to engineering. Finally, some newly-emerging issues in the area of imbalanced learning are discussed.
